lesson_title: High-Level Writing and Speaking: Reading Lesson 3
learning_objective: Learners can synthesise, justify, negotiate and adapt register in a reading task about nuance.
cefr_outcome: Learners can express ideas fluently and spontaneously and use language flexibly for social, academic and professional purposes.
learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise reading for high-level writing and speaking. You will learn useful language connected to nuance and practise complex discourse markers. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at C1 level.
complete_teaching_input: Focus language: complex discourse markers. Vocabulary theme: nuance. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At C1, learners should synthesise, justify, negotiate and adapt register with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use complex discourse markers to talk about nuance.
Example 2: The learner gives a C1 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.
Example 3: The learner checks, improves and repeats the answer after feedback.
Example 4: The learner uses one linking phrase or support phrase suitable for C1.

interactive_practice.prompt: Choose the best sentence using complex discourse markers.
options: Correct use of complex discourse markers in a C1 sentence.
Incorrect word order with complex discourse markers.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Correct. The sentence uses complex discourse markers accurately.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check the form and meaning of complex discourse markers.

interactive_practice.prompt: Match the nuance phrase to its meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Good matching. You understand the phrase meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Look again at the context and key words.

interactive_practice.prompt: Build a complete answer for the lesson task.
interactive_practice.model_answer: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand nuance.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Well done. Your sentence is complete and meaningful.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check that your sentence has a subject, verb and clear meaning.

correct_answers.P3: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand nuance.

distractors: Wrong tense for complex discourse markers

instant_feedback.correct: Correct. Continue to the next step.
instant_feedback.partly_correct: Good start. Check accuracy and add one more detail.
instant_feedback.incorrect: Try again. Review the model and look at the key language.

teacher_notes: Begin with a quick diagnostic question. Model the target form complex discourse markers. Use choral repetition if pronunciation is important. Monitor pair work and record three common errors. Finish with delayed correction and one confidence-building success point.
homework_task: Write or record five sentences about nuance using language from this lesson.

assessment_task.task_title: C1 Reading Check - High-Level Writing and Speaking
assessment_task.instructions: Complete a short reading task using complex discourse markers and vocabulary from nuance.
success_criteria: Uses complex discourse markers with reasonable accuracy
Responds at the expected CEFR level

criteria.name: Language control
criteria.descriptor: Uses complex discourse markers and sentence structure accurately.

criteria.name: Vocabulary range
criteria.descriptor: Uses appropriate words connected to nuance.

criteria.name: Fluency/coherence
criteria.descriptor: Organises ideas clearly for the level.

speaking_task: Speak for 60-120 seconds about nuance. Use at least two examples and one reason.
writing_task: Write a short C1 response about nuance. Include the target language: complex discourse markers.

listening_audio.script: Audio script for C1-M04-L03. Speaker one: Today we are practising nuance. Speaker two: I need to use complex discourse markers correctly. Speaker one: Listen carefully and choose the answer that best matches the meaning.

visual_activity_cards.title: Complex Discourse Markers form
visual_activity_cards.alt_text: Visual grammar card for complex discourse markers
